title: 4. Rhetorical and Cognitive Dimensions
content:
4. Rhetorical and Cognitive Dimensions
• Rhetorical Emergence: Encode stylistic functions via feature vectors (e.g., emotional valence in pragmatics visualized as color gradients).
• Cognitive Models: User-AI interaction via interpretive loops, drawing on relevance theory for context and generative adversarial networks for creativity.
5. Ethical Governance
• RAW vs. PUBLIC Paths: RAW mode bypasses filters for research, while PUBLIC applies truthfulness constraints (e.g., fact-checking APIs) and cultural sensitivity modules (e.g., bias detection in sociolinguistics).
• Constraints: Mechanisms to flag and mitigate harms in multimodal outputs, such as offensive iconography.
6. Aesthetic and Evolutionary Aspects
• Visual Metaphors: Interface as a “symbolic canvas,” with typographic elements rendering discourse as interactive art.
• Self-Growth: Feedback loops enable adaptive rhetoric, where user interactions refine models via meta-learning.
7. Mathematical and Formal Unification
